title: OCP Microscaling Formats (MX) v1.0 Specification
preview: Industry standard specification defining MX4, MX6, and MX9 microscaling number formats adopted by AMD, Intel, Microsoft, NVIDIA, and Qualcomm for AI hardware.
summary: Defines the Microscaling (MX) format family, which uses a shared block-level scale factor paired with narrow mantissa elements (FP4, FP6, FP8, INT8) to achieve higher numeric range than pure low-bit formats. MX formats are the hardware basis for next-generation low-precision AI compute in H100 follow-ons and AMD MI350+.
datacenter: Critical standard for AI hardware procurement and software stack planning. MX formats (especially MXFP4) will be the primary precision type in next-generation training and inference silicon, determining which models can be efficiently deployed on post-Blackwell hardware.
metrics: Key result signal: quantization accuracy parity with FP16 using MXFP4 on standard LLM benchmarks validated by consortium members.
